summary: Deprecated audit trail for the mixed cross-device preset model. The documented EnableCdp machine policy is split into a child record; the user-side CDP preset values remain unresolved and are tracked only as implementation detail here.

setting.name: Cross-Device Experiences
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can let devices belonging to the same user discover each other and continue some experiences across devices. The current app tries to offer Off, My devices only, and Everyone nearby presets.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off for privacy or to avoid device discoverability, while others keep it on for cross-device convenience.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces discoverability and continue experiences. The current product issue is that only the machine policy part is officially documented; the per-user preset values need stronger sourcing.
setting.professional_notes: GroupPolicy.admx documents HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnableCdp with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The app additionally writes per-user CDP values under CurrentVersion\CDP and SettingsPage, but those preset mappings are not yet backed by an official source in this record.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The device follows the Windows edition default for continue experiences.

allowed_values.label: Continue experiences blocked
allowed_values.meaning: The device is not discoverable by other devices and cannot participate in cross-device experiences.

allowed_values.label: Continue experiences allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The device can participate in cross-device experiences.

allowed_values.label: Observed app off preset
allowed_values.meaning: Current app preset uses 0 for the user-side CDP values, but an official mapping is not yet attached in this record.

allowed_values.label: Observed app my-devices preset
allowed_values.meaning: Current app preset uses 1 for the user-side CDP values, but an official mapping is not yet attached in this record.

allowed_values.label: Observed app everyone-nearby preset
allowed_values.meaning: Current app preset uses 2 for the user-side CDP values, but an official mapping is not yet attached in this record.

targets.notes: These user-side preset values are observed in the current app implementation, but they are not yet officially sourced in this record.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app mixes the official EnableCdp device policy with undocumented user-side CDP preset values to offer three presets.

windows_defaults.label: Windows mixed-scope baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 and newer systems exposing Continue Experiences policy

states.rationale: Microsoft documents the device-policy default as depending on the Windows edition.

windows_defaults.notes: The user-side CDP values still need authoritative sourcing before a reliable default model can be published.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems where cross-device requirements are unknown
avoid_for: Environments that want a strict policy block

states.rationale: Leave the official machine policy unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the documented Windows edition default.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a clear and fully sourced preset for cross-device behavior.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its ordinary policy baseline.
The preset-style user values remain unresolved.

recommended_profiles.label: Candidate off preset
intended_for: Users who want continue experiences turned off
avoid_for: Anyone expecting the preset semantics to be fully official today

states.rationale: Observed app preset value pending stronger sourcing.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You understand this is still a research-stage preset.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a fully sourced and publishable preset model.
tradeoffs: The official machine-policy part is clear.
The user-side preset values are still under research.
recommended_profiles.notes: Candidate profile only until the per-user values are officially sourced.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_GroupPolicy Policy CSP: EnableCDP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the machine policy that controls whether the device can participate in continue experiences.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.admx EnableCDP mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps EnableCdp to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnableCdp with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.adml EnableCDP help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains that enabling the policy allows continue experiences, disabling it blocks them, and the not-configured default depends on the Windows edition.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider mixes the official EnableCdp policy with user-side CDP registry values to model Off, My devices only, and Everyone nearby presets.

decision.why: The official machine policy is clear, but the current preset model depends on additional per-user values that are not yet officially sourced in this record.
blocking_issues: The preset model mixes one official machine policy with undocumented user-side CDP values.
The product should not present fully trusted preset semantics until those user values are sourced and validated.

open_questions: Are the user-side CDP values fully official somewhere, or should they be treated as observed UI preference data only?
Should this experience be split into a simple official machine policy toggle plus a separate advanced per-user preset model?

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.adml EnableCDP help text: The local ADML help text explains that enabling the policy allows continue experiences, disabling it blocks them, and the not-configured default depends on the Windows edition.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from evidence_id local-grouppolicy-adml-enablecdp (official-doc); deprecated audit trail.
